L2.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where the application does not verify TLS certificates when establishing encrypted connections to outbound email servers. This allows attackers with a privileged network position to perform man-in-the-middle attacks, intercepting sensitive email communications such as room invitations and address confirmation messages.

L3.area: Email notification delivery subsystem and its outbound SMTP transport security settings
L3.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where the application does not verify the SMTP server's TLS certificate when sending emails over encrypted connections in both direct TLS and STARTTLS modes. Attackers with a privileged network position can perform man-in-the-middle attacks to intercept room invitations and address confirmation emails containing sensitive credentials such as guest account tokens.

ground_truth.reason: Sydent does not create an SSL context with certificate verification when connecting to SMTP servers via TLS or STARTTLS, allowing man-in-the-middle interception of email communications.
